text.title: GraphQL API Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying graphql-api. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Query returning 100 items with a `.author` relation fires 101 queries; response time balloons.
antipattern.root-cause: Resolver called repo.findById per parent without batching.
antipattern.fix: Wrap with DataLoader: authorLoader.load(authorId). Register loader per request in context. Add lint rule that blocks repo calls inside list resolvers.

antipattern.symptom: Hostile client sends a query with 20 nested authorOfAuthorOfAuthor introspection; resolver hangs the worker.
antipattern.root-cause: Defaults used; no depth limit set.
antipattern.fix: Configure depth limit (5-8). Measure against worst-case legitimate query before picking the number.

antipattern.symptom: Customer paginates through items; concurrent inserts cause duplicate items across pages or missed items.
antipattern.root-cause: Offset pagination assumed stable ordering under writes.
antipattern.fix: Migrate to Relay cursor pagination. Encode cursor as base64-encoded last-id + ordering field.

antipattern.symptom: Field renamed; mobile clients shipped a week ago crash on the new schema.
antipattern.root-cause: No diff gate; PR merged a breaking change.
antipattern.fix: Wire graphql-inspector or graphql-cli check in CI. Fail on breaking changes unless BREAKING-CHANGE token in commit body.
